title: Keyless keyword preview + best-path ranker under-score an athlete — regexes are scientist/founder-shaped (no coach/podium/championship/title/sponsorship)
expected: The instant-verdict preview and the best-path comparison should credit an athlete's strong evidence — World Cup podiums + national titles -> Awards, national-team -> Membership, coaching the squad to medals -> Critical role, sponsorships -> High remuneration — well enough that O-1A surfaces as a sensible path.
got: The mock scores by keyword regex (qualification.ts:225-248 over packs.ts). Awards matches only 'medal'/'won' (NOT 'podium'/'championship'/'title'); Critical role's regex has no 'coach'/'coaching'; High remuneration's regex has no 'sponsorship'. So an athlete can score ~2/8 on the preview and the best-path ranker (best-path.ts:76, mock-only, no model fallback), omitting two of his genuinely strong criteria. The hero SoftGate honestly labels it a keyword read and routes to the deep model screening (InstantVerdict.tsx:273-276); the best-path finder has no equally prominent caveat.
suggested_acceptance: Either (a) widen the Awards/Critical-role/Remuneration regexes to include athletic terms (podium, championship, title, coach/coaching, sponsor/sponsorship, national team), or (b) add a best-path caveat as prominent as the hero SoftGate that the comparison is a keyword pass and the model screening reads deeper. The authenticated /api/qualify (full CV to the real model) is unaffected.
l2_priority: Run Marcus's athlete profile through the LIVE /api/qualify and confirm the real model maps podiums/titles->Awards, national-team->Membership, coaching->Critical role, sponsorships->Remuneration as Met/Strong, and Scholarly articles as None; then compare against what the keyword best-path shows him.

title: 'Certificate of Extraordinary Ability / Qualifies / Approved' ceremony framing on the hero + shared card
expected: A low-BS, results-driven coach wants a sober screening read, not award-ceremony branding that risks a 'horoscope' feel.
got: The hero and /c/[token] wrap the honest scored data in a Certificate/Seal/Stamp ceremony. The underlying data is honest (statuses, threshold, disclaimer all present); only the branding is theatrical. This is the already-tracked backlog item G3.1 / PN-QUAL-01 (polish, uncertain/subjective).
scope_note: Tracked open backlog item G3.1/PN-QUAL-01; not a new defect. Recorded only because this Character (a blunt, results-driven coach) is precisely the persona the backlog flags as 'rigor-focused, risks the horoscope read'. Defer to L2's credibility judgement.
suggested_acceptance: Per G3.1: optionally soften 'Approved/Certificate' toward 'informational screening' while keeping the scored data. No change required for L1.
l2_priority: Judge live whether the ceremony framing aids or hurts credibility for a low-BS athlete.

title: Draft grounds on per-criterion evidence/rationale (+ vault exhibits), not the raw pasted CV — so athlete specifics depend on what qualify captured
expected: The draft should name Marcus's real podiums, national titles, the squad's medals, and his sponsors by name.
got: buildDraftPrompt is fed the criteria's evidence+rationale strings (drafting.ts:148-150,196-199 via criterionLine) plus vault exhibits (draftOperation.ts:160-161), NOT req.profile. The qualify model captures the user's specifics into each criterion's evidence at screening time, and that becomes the draft's argument. This is the accepted, RESOLVED backlog item G1.3 / PN-DRAFT-01 (no code change; L2 previously proved the live draft names supplied specifics for a researcher).
scope_note: Accepted gap G1.3/PN-DRAFT-01 (resolved, no code change). Suppressed as a defect per scope-honesty; recorded as the athlete-specific L2 watch item because the prior L2 proof was on a researcher profile (FoldX2/RECOMB/patent#), not athletic evidence.
suggested_acceptance: None for L1. L2 must verify the live draft names the athlete's specifics.
l2_priority: Generate Marcus's live draft and assert it names his real IFSC World Cup podiums, national titles, the junior squad's medals, and his named sponsors — not generic 'the record reflects an award'.

title: No athlete/coach profession in the programmatic-SEO matrix; criterion examples are scientist/founder/artist-shaped
expected: For O-1A athletics, profession-tuned examples (podiums->Awards, national team->Membership, coaching->Critical role, sponsorship->Remuneration) would help an athlete recognize his fit.
got: professions.ts ships only software-engineer, researcher, founder, designer, artist (professions.ts:28,45,62,79,98). An athlete hitting a /visa/O-1A/<profession> SEO page would see founder/scientist evidence examples. NOT on Marcus's three journeys (he uses the generic paste flow), so it blocks nothing — but the marketing surface doesn't anticipate athletes.
suggested_acceptance: Add an 'athlete-coach' Profession entry with athletic criterion examples so the SEO/landing surface covers O-1A athletics. Off Marcus's core path; low priority.
l2_priority: Optional — confirm a /visa/O-1A/athlete-style landing renders sensible examples if such a profession is added.

title: Qualify prompt forbids fabrication AND cross-criterion padding; 'None' renders neutral via single-source threshold math
expected: An honest read marks thin criteria None (never green, never invented) and doesn't let one criterion's evidence satisfy another.
got: buildQualifyPrompt rule 2 forbids inventing facts/awards/publications; rule 4 forbids cross-criterion padding (qualification.ts:132-138). statusTone('None')='neutral' and summarizeCriteria ignores None toward the >=3 threshold, single-sourced via classifyStatus (criteria.ts:27-95). Live legalAdviceGate + disclaimerGate enforce UPL at runtime (adjudication-gates.ts:135-142,204-209,302-303), surfaced as AdjudicationBadge (QualifyPanel.tsx:269).
suggested_acceptance: Protect this wording and the single-source status math; do not soften.
l2_priority: Confirm the live read marks Scholarly articles None (grey) and flags Press as a gap, without force-fitting either.

title: Letter sections only Met/Strong criteria + live fabrication/UPL gates — structurally cannot manufacture a paper or a podium
expected: The draft argues only the criteria he genuinely meets and invents zero podiums/titles/papers/sponsor figures.
got: mockDraft and the prompt generate one section ONLY per 'Met'/'Strong' criterion (drafting.ts:83-85,204-205,665) — a 'None' scholarly criterion gets no section, so there's nothing to pad. STRICT RULES forbid inventing awards/dates/counts (drafting.ts:181-192). Live fabricationGate flags any output number/year/money not traceable to the record (adjudication-gates.ts:122-127,212-217,300); citation audit quarantines hallucinated (Exhibit N) (drafting.ts:593-608).
suggested_acceptance: Protect the Met/Strong-only sectioning and the live gates.
l2_priority: Confirm the live athlete draft contains no Scholarly-Articles section and the fabrication gate stays clean on his real numbers.

title: Share token leaks zero profile text, is DB-free, tamper-rejecting, and keeps statuses honest on the card
expected: A shareable verdict that exposes nothing private and reads honest, not overclaiming.
got: encodeSnapshot carries only name/classification/likelihood/statuses, never the profile (letters-patent.ts:70-77). /c/[token] page + OG render purely from decodeSnapshot, no DB (page.tsx:45, opengraph-image.tsx:25). decodeSnapshot rejects a token whose status count mismatches the pack (letters-patent.ts:93-97). Criteria badges reuse statusTone so 'None' stays neutral on the card (page.tsx:104); stamp says 'Qualifies' only above threshold and is labelled 'not legal advice' (page.tsx:119,126).
suggested_acceptance: Protect the token codec's leak-free, DB-free, tamper-rejecting design.
l2_priority: Render Marcus's share link + OG card live and confirm no private data and an honest verdict.
